rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Defendants Motion To Terminate Receivership
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 25, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANTs ANNE KIHAGI, JULIA MWANGI, XELAN PROP 1, LLC, RENKA PROP, LLC, NOZARI 2, LLC, ZORIALL, LLC, and CHRISTINE MWANGI's Amended Motion To Terminate Receivership is OFF CALENDAR. Noticed for May 28, 2021. = (501/VP) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: OASIS GRILL, LLC ET AL VS. ERIC KETCHUM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 25, 2021 line 4. PLAINTIFFs OASIS GRILL, LLC and MOHAMMAD ZUGHAIYER's Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint is GRANTED as follows: (1) trial date is vacated; (2) Plaintiff shall file its supplemental (cause of action for constructive eviction is based on events occurring after the complaint was filed. See Reply 1:1-4) and amended complaint within 2 court days of execution of the order; (3) discovery remains open until 30 days before the new trial date for defendant to conduct written discovery and additional depositions regarding new causes of action for constructive eviction, nuisance and UCL violations; (4) Plaintiff shall prepare and provide for execution a proposed order in conformity with this ruling by 9:00 a.m. on May 25, 2021. = (501/VP) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SALLY L. LIU VS. BRUCE R. BEASLEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 25, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANT BRIAN GETZ's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of each cause of action in clear and concise language. Plaintiff shall also allege facts in support of her claim that no tender is required to quiet title of the original loan amount and/or interest. Plaintiff shall further allege facts showing that her claims arise out of foreclosure activities as opposed as to any wrongdoing in the origination of the loan to address the statute of limitations. = (501/VP) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: SALLY L. LIU VS. BRUCE R. BEASLEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Striking Brian Getzs Demurrer To Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint In Its Entirety And Request For Entry Of Default Against Defendant Brian Getz; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Sally L. Liu
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 25, 2021 line 5. PLAINTIFF SALLY LIU's Motion For Order Striking Brian Getzs Demurrer To Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint In Its Entirety And Request For Entry Of Default Against Defendant Brian Getz is DENIED. While demurrer was filed more than 35 after service and there was no declaration of extension on file (e.g. CIV-141) there was no request for default on file preventing the calendaring of the demurrer. Additionally, the demurrer was set exactly 16 court days out without any additional time to account for service under CCP 1013; 1010.6. However, demurrer was opposed on the merits and striking the demurrer would only cause an unnecessary delay in the matter. = (501/VP) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
